ps140: O blessed Job,* you were known to be righteous and guileless,* truthful, blameless and faithful,* abstaining from every evil thing,* truly adorned with the virtues* and resplendent in piety.* Therefore, we call you blessed* and, rejoicing, we celebrate today* your holy and honoured festival.

Arrayed in the riches of piety* as in a costly robe of purple,* and adorned with mercy and righteousness* as with a divine crown,* you exercised dominion over the tyrannical passions,* O blameless Job;* therefore, you have passed over to the noetic kingdom,* to stand before the eternal King,* O all-glorious one.

Let us praise Job,* the blameless favourite of the Lord,* who, amid his trials,* was unconquered by the sores of his flesh,* the unshaken pillar,* who was recognized by all as a bulwark of courage,* who with his wisdom-loving mind broke the sting of the devil,* the godly and divinely wise one,* the confirmation of the pious.
